epigraph.text: For every complex problem there is an answer that is clear, simple, and wrong.

follyOfTheDay.headline: Robber's Incriminating Headwear Enables Hour-Long Manhunt
follyOfTheDay.summary: In a demonstration of criminal ingenuity so spectacularly compromised it borders on the theatrical, a Michigan convenience store robber selected the establishment's own security camera as his preferred haberdashery. Security footage documents his acquisition of several hundred dollars—and comprehensive photographic documentation of his every move—before his rapid apprehension. Police expressed bewilderment at the suspect's apparent belief that wearing the very technology designed to capture his likeness would somehow shield his identity. The camera, mounted securely atop his head in what witnesses described as a "jaunty fashion," provided law enforcement with both angle and temporal data. The suspect was located and arrested within one hour of the incident. Store management confirmed all footage was recoverable.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: In the annals of criminal stupidity, few achievements rival the suspect who volunteered to document his own malfeasance with broadcast-quality resolution.
